testCases.notes: Search by name (Emily → 1 row) and by email (test-david.kim-... → 1 row) both filter correctly via Enter key. Org has 4 members (precondition specified 5+); search filter functionality verified regardless.

testCases.title: Members List Pagination
testCases.notes: Precondition not met: AGENT-TEST-Org-TechFlow has only 4 members (Emily, David, John + 1 invite). Pagination requires >10 members. Cannot meaningfully exercise pagination/load-more behavior.

Invite member modal opens with Role (Org employee/Purchasing agent/Org maintainer dropdown), multi-email textarea, and optional Message. First/last name fields NOT in invite form — by design, names are filled by invitee on acceptance. Test case spec called for first/last name fields; this is a test-spec vs actual-design mismatch, not a defect.

Invitation sent for AGENT-TEST-mbr005-1777969024@test-agent.com with role 'Purchasing agent'. New row appeared at top of list with status 'Invite sent' / 'Invited'. inviteUser GraphQL mutation returned succeeded:true, errors:[]. Cleanup: deleted invitation at end of suite.

InviteUser mutation payload includes urlSuffix:'/confirm-invitation' indicating email notification with confirmation link. Mutation returned succeeded:true, empty errors array. Email delivery verification requires Admin SPA access (out of scope for storefront-only test).

testCases.title: Invite Member - New Member Sign-In via Invitation Link
testCases.notes: Requires admin SPA access to retrieve invitation link from notifications log. Per memory reference_email_verification_workflow.md: in QA env, the full flow requires Admin Security→User→Resend→extract link from Notifications list. Outside storefront-only regression scope.

Edit role workflow: Actions menu (3-dot) → Edit role → modal with radio buttons → save. Changed David Kim from Purchasing agent → Organization maintainer. UI updated immediately. Cleanup: reverted to Purchasing agent.

RBAC verified end-to-end. As Organization maintainer (John Mitchell): Invite Members button visible + Actions menu (Edit role/Block/Delete) per row. As Purchasing agent (David Kim): Invite Members button NOT in DOM, no Actions buttons (DOM count = 0). Confirms role-based UI gating per BL-B2C-004.

Block flow: Actions → Block user → confirmation modal (Are you sure you want to block access...) → OK. Status changed to Blocked (red minus icon). Login attempt as David Kim was rejected; sign-in page displayed message: 'You have been blocked. You can not reach any information or create the order. For more details please contact the site administrator.' No access token issued, URL stayed at /sign-in.

Unblock flow: Actions → Unblock user → confirmation → OK. Status reverted to Active (green check). Login attempt as David Kim succeeded; access token issued; redirected to /. Member regained org context.

Status badges visually distinct: Active (green check), Blocked (red minus), Invited (blue dots), Inactive (gray check). All status icons have alt text matching displayed status. Format passes [DOM] and [FORMAT] assertions.

testCases.title: Delegated Purchasing - Purchaser Order Goes to Approval
testCases.notes: Approval workflow not configured for AGENT-TEST-Org-TechFlow-20260310. GraphQL `organization` query shows dynamicProperties = [Authorize Net Profile Id, IsTermsAgreement, Sales Rep Code, SkipCardPreauthorization] — no approval threshold or workflow flag. Storefront `/company/info` and `/account/*` paths show no approval-related UI or links. Test data setup required: org-level approval threshold + approver role assignment.

testCases.title: Delegated Purchasing - Approver Sees Pending Orders
testCases.notes: Same as B2C-MBR-013: no approval workflow configured for TechFlow org. No 'Pending approvals' section in storefront sidebar/account UI. Cannot exercise approver flow without backend setup.

testCases.title: Delegated Purchasing - Approve Order
testCases.notes: Same blocker as B2C-MBR-013/014: approval workflow not configured. Requires pre-existing pending order which cannot be created without threshold setup.

testCases.title: Delegated Purchasing - Reject Order
testCases.notes: Same blocker as B2C-MBR-013/014/015. No approval workflow configured for test org.

testCases.title: Delegated Purchasing - Amount Threshold Triggers Approval
testCases.notes: Same blocker as B2C-MBR-013-016. No approval threshold configured on test org. Cannot test threshold boundary enforcement without backend setup.

testCases.title: Company Members Count Display
testCases.failedAssertion: [DOM] Member count shown on page (header or footer)
testCases.notes: No member count is displayed on /company/members page. Heading shows only 'Company members'. Table caption is just 'Company members'. Body regex /(\d+)\s*members?/i returns null. With 5 visible rows (4 active members + 1 invitation), users cannot see total count without manually counting. Pairs with B2C-MBR-003 (pagination): both indicate missing member-count UX feature.

bugs.title: Company Members page lacks member count display
bugs.failedAssertion: [DOM] Member count shown on page (header or footer)
bugs.stepsToReproduce: 1. Sign in as Org admin (Organization maintainer). 2. Navigate to /company/members. 3. Inspect page header, body, footer for a member count.
bugs.expected: Member count visible (e.g., 'Company members (5)' or 'Showing 5 of 5'). Count matches visible rows.
bugs.actual: No count anywhere on page. Heading and table caption say only 'Company members'.
